project_reference.core_research_question: Can Awl's bounded local Ollama dispatch workflow produce defensible frontier-token savings in real Claude/Codex coding sessions without unacceptable pass-rate or reliability regressions?
project_reference.current_focus: Phase 1 ready to plan: reliability and measurement patches.

scope.question: Can Awl's bounded local Ollama dispatch workflow produce defensible frontier-token savings in real Claude/Codex coding sessions without unacceptable pass-rate or reliability regressions?
in_scope: Ship the confirmed reliability patches needed before Step 1 measurement resumes: one default apply+verify attempt, per-dispatch model override, failure-category telemetry, and input/output token cost accounting.
Resume and complete the Step 1 A/B savings experiment using separate 7B-only and 14B-only Awl configurations plus a direct frontier baseline.
Produce frontier-side guidance for when opting up from 7B to 14B is justified by measured pass rate, failure category, and token-savings evidence.
out_of_scope: Adding Python preflight, streaming dispatch output, dispatch caching, or per-task local-token ceilings before Step 1 demands them.
Changing experiment task definitions to make existing tasks easier.
Replacing the OpenAI-compatible JSON-schema response format without explicit user authorization.
Relaxing CI, lint, branch-protection, or local-only worker constraints.
unresolved_questions: What direct-frontier baseline token data should populate experiments/results/baseline.csv?
Which additional tasks should extend experiments/tasks/ to at least 10 mixed Python/Rust tasks without biasing the benchmark?
Does 14B improve pass rate enough to justify slower local execution and frontier session overhead?
Cargo.toml and README.md indicate MIT licensing while HANDOFF_TO_GPD.md says AGPL-3.0; resolve before release or publication metadata is cited.

context_gaps: No experiments/results/baseline.csv frontier baseline was found.
Original AwlUsageReport.md and reportreport.txt are cited by UPDATED_PROGRESS_REPORT.md but are not present in this workspace.
crucial_inputs: Never push directly to main; use branch -> PR -> CI -> merge.
Never run destructive git operations or skip pre-commit hooks without explicit same-session user confirmation.
Required CI checks remain checks (ubuntu-latest) and checks (macos-latest).
All new public Rust functions need at least one unit test.
Ollama must remain optional at compile time; unreachable Ollama returns structured dispatch errors instead of crashing.

formulations: Empirical A/B comparison between direct frontier implementation and Awl-assisted bounded local dispatch.
Reliability-first preparation before Step 1 measurement resumes.
allowed_estimator_families: Direct per-task frontier input/output token accounting from baseline.csv and Awl telemetry.
Pass-rate and failure-category aggregation from local dispatch results.
forbidden_estimator_families: Qualitative impressions of local model quality without frontier baseline comparison.
Blended single-rate cost accounting after input/output token split is implemented.
allowed_fit_families: Aggregate and per-configuration comparison of 7B-only and 14B-only results against the same frontier baseline.
forbidden_fit_families: Post hoc task exclusions that remove hard failures from the benchmark.
Tuning benchmark tasks to be easier for 7B.
stop_and_rethink_conditions: CI fails for a non-obvious reason.
A PR changes the dispatch contract visible to callers or experiment definitions before user confirmation.
Step 1 cannot produce both a comparable baseline and same-task Awl configurations.
A result appears to show savings only by counting proxy metrics rather than direct frontier-token reduction.

observables.name: Frontier-token reduction
observables.definition: Aggregate percent reduction in paid frontier input/output tokens for Awl-assisted workflow versus direct frontier baseline across the same task pack.

observables.name: Awl pass rate
observables.definition: Fraction of attempted benchmark tasks that pass the task verifier under a given Awl model configuration.

observables.name: Dispatch failure category distribution
observables.definition: Distribution of dispatch failures over format, schema, preflight, verify, timeout, network, and unknown categories.

observables.name: Input/output token cost split
observables.definition: Cost estimate computed from prompt/input and completion/output tokens using separate per-MTok rates.

claims.statement: Step 1 will produce either a defensible positive result that at least one Awl configuration achieves at least 25% frontier-token reduction at at least 60% Awl pass rate, or a defensible negative result with documented blockers.
The architectural patches needed before resuming Step 1 are implemented without weakening dispatch safety, local-only execution, lint, CI, or branch-protection constraints.

deliverables.description: Independently shippable patches for retry default, per-dispatch model override, failure-category telemetry, and input/output cost accounting.
must_contain: src/dispatch.rs retry default change and tests
DispatchOptions model override through CLI and MCP schema
failure_category in apply_result and error_result telemetry
input/output token cost flags in experiments/tally.py and scripts/dispatch_cost_report.py

deliverables.description: Step 1 A/B savings report comparing 7B-only and 14B-only Awl configurations against the direct frontier baseline.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-step1-answer
acceptance_tests.procedure: Run the expanded task pack through 7B-only Awl, 14B-only Awl, and direct frontier baseline, then tally token reduction and pass rate using split input/output token costs.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Report identifies at least one configuration with >=25% frontier-token reduction and >=60% Awl pass rate, or documents a negative result with concrete blockers and no unsupported savings claim.

acceptance_tests.subject: claim-reliability-prep
acceptance_tests.procedure: For each reliability patch PR, run cargo fmt, cargo test, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, and required GitHub CI checks without weakening existing gates.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All required local and GitHub CI checks pass, new public Rust functions have tests, and no forbidden dispatch-contract or experiment-definition change proceeds without user confirmation.

references.why_it_matters: User-supplied project map with mission, hard constraints, required reading order, work queue, and stop conditions.
Current research artifact and patch list; records the deterministic 7B failure, user-confirmed retry/model decisions, and Step 1 continuation plan.
Historical context for why controlled token-savings measurement is now justified but not yet proven.
Defines the benchmark harness, baseline collection procedure, task constraints, and pass/fail thresholds.
Only current benchmark data: 3 attempted tasks, 2 passes, and 01_string_helper deterministic trailing-newline failure after same-model retry.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-step1-answer
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Treating local model pass rate or qualitative local model quality as evidence of frontier-token savings without direct frontier baseline comparison.
forbidden_proxies.reason: The product hypothesis is paid frontier-token savings, not local model quality in isolation.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-step1-answer
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Changing benchmark tasks to make 7B pass more often.
forbidden_proxies.reason: This would bias the savings experiment and erase the signal from capability gaps.

forbidden_proxies.subject: claim-reliability-prep
forbidden_proxies.proxy: Suppressing lints, tests, pre-commit hooks, or CI failures to make reliability patches appear complete.
forbidden_proxies.reason: Passing safety and verification gates is part of the deliverable, not incidental cleanup.

weakest_anchors: No direct frontier baseline data exists yet; experiments/results/baseline.csv must be collected before any savings claim is credible.
The 14B-only configuration is proposed but unmeasured.
unvalidated_assumptions: Expanded task pack can represent real bounded coding work without being tuned to either model.
Frontier token accounting will be comparable across baseline and Awl-assisted sessions.
competing_explanations: Any observed token savings may come from task selection or accounting differences rather than Awl's local-worker value.
14B may improve pass rate but lose on total workflow cost or latency.
disconfirming_observations: No Awl configuration reaches >=25% frontier-token reduction at >=60% pass rate on the same task pack.
Savings disappear when input and output token costs are split correctly.
Failures concentrate in verify or schema categories that force expensive frontier recovery.

position.last_activity: Project initialized; roadmap and conventions established.

open_questions: What direct-frontier baseline token data should populate experiments/results/baseline.csv?
Which additional tasks should extend experiments/tasks/ to at least 10 mixed Python/Rust tasks without biasing the benchmark?
Does 14B improve pass rate enough to justify slower local execution and frontier session overhead?
Cargo.toml and README.md indicate MIT licensing while HANDOFF_TO_GPD.md says AGPL-3.0; resolve before release or publication metadata is cited.

handoff.stopped_at: Project initialized; ready for Phase 1 planning.
